title: oenothera macrocarpa subsp. oklahomensis
intro: Oenothera macrocarpa subsp. oklahomensis, also known as Oklahoma evening primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the evening primrose family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The medicinal properties of Oklahoma evening primrose have been attributed to its high concentration of carotenoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving wound healing. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically to promote skin health and aid in the recovery of skin conditions.
